tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Used by:** PR checkout with `--sync` flag — appends `gt submit --no-interactive` so the branch is submitted to Graphite after checkout navigation.
**Used by:** PR teleport with `--sync` flag — appends `gt submit --no-interactive` so the branch is submitted to Graphite after teleport navigation.

tool_use.name: Edit

**Example:** PR teleport with `--sync` flag passes `["gt submit --no-interactive"]` as `po...[truncated 90 chars]...k/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py` uses the `post_cd_commands` parameter when `sync=True`.

session.assistant: Now update the cmux skill file:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
cmux new-workspace --command 'source "$(erk pr checkout 456 --script --sync)"'
# WRONG - ...[truncated 4 chars]...ent shell cmux new-workspace --command "source \"$(erk pr checkout 456 --script --sync)\""

cmux new-workspace --command 'source "$(erk pr teleport 456 --new-slot --script --sync)"' ...[truncated 26 chars]...mux new-workspace --command "source \"$(erk pr teleport 456 --new-slot --script --sync)\""

session.tool_result: <tool_use_error>File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.</tool_use_error>

150→### Shell quoting with `--command` 151→ 152→Use single quotes to prevent outer shell expansion of `$()`: 153→ 154→```bash 155→# CORRECT - subshell runs inside workspace 156→cmux new-workspace --command 'source "$(erk pr checkout 456 --script --sync)"' 157→ 158→# WRONG - subshell expands in current shell 159→cmux new-workspace --command "source \"$(erk pr checkout 456 --script --sync)\"" 160→``` 161→ 162→### No `--working-directory` CLI flag 163→ 164→The CLI does not expose a working directory flag. Workaround: 165→ 166→```bash 167→cmux new-workspace --command 'cd /path/to/project' 168→``` 169→ 170→### Startup delay 171→ 172→There is a ~0.5s internal delay after workspace creation before commands are sent, to allow the shell to initialize. Keep this in mind when chaining commands. 173→ 174→### Workspace names are just labels 175→ 176→cmux knows nothing about git branches. Branch names used as workspace titles via `rename-workspace` are purely cosmetic labels for human navigation. 177→ 178→## Erk Integration 179→
... [45 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.param:

cmux new-workspace --command 'source "$(erk pr teleport 456 --new-slot --script --sync)"' ...[truncated 26 chars]...mux new-workspace --command "source \"$(erk pr teleport 456 --new-slot --script --sync)\""

The test `test_execute_split_plan_creates_worktrees` is **failing** due to a pre-existing issue. The error shows:
- `AttributeError: 'FakeGit' object has no attribute 'worktree'` - The test's `FakeGit` mock object doesn't have the `worktree` attribute that the code expects - The code at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/stack/split_old/plan.py:207` calls `git_ops.worktree.add_worktree()`, but the test's fake doesn't provide this nested structure
